layer_map.what: Receives Divider — the one genuinely domain-agnostic shared primitive; its two consumers become legal organism→molecule downward imports
Shell-domain group; PaneSplit co-locates with its sole consumer Shell (props carry request/response vocabulary → cannot live in the agnostic molecules tier)
Shell test trio moves intact (relative `./Shell.stories` coupling)
Domain singletons stay; Sidebar rewrites only its Divider import
Untouched — no move, no import rewrite

key_design_decisions.why: Divider is domain-agnostic (imports only `./Divider.css`, react, `@renderer/lib/cx`; pure-primitive props; 2 consumers). Move makes PaneSplit→Divider and Sidebar→Divider legal organism→molecule downward imports, clearing the live §2.2:56 sibling-tier violation. Enforces the existing canon. (AC-2)
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: Keep in organisms/: leaves the §2.2:56 violation live and mis-tiers a shared primitive

key_design_decisions.why: Public contract carries domain vocabulary (props `request?`/`response?`, docstring "request/response vertical split", sole consumer Shell.tsx:80). molecules/ would breach the agnostic-tier invariant, and a zero-change move cannot strip the binding. Joins its consumer in the shell group. (AC-3)

key_design_decisions.chosen_approach: All three (`Shell.test.tsx`, `Shell.stories.tsx`, `Shell.ct.tsx`) → new `organisms/shell/__tests__/`; TabBar.test.tsx stays in `organisms/__tests__/`
key_design_decisions.why: `Shell.ct.tsx` imports `./Shell.stories` by RELATIVE path — splitting the trio breaks that coupling. Co-locating tests at the new folder level follows the tests-next-to-code convention. (AC-7, constraint "move co-located __tests__ together")
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: Leave trio in `organisms/__tests__/`: dangles the relative import or forces a §2.3-discouraged deep relative path

key_design_decisions.chosen_approach: None — at any of molecules/, organisms/, organisms/shell/
key_design_decisions.why: Re-export tax, worse tree-shaking, cycle risk; `@renderer` already gives clean paths. Codebase has zero barrel files today — continuing is consistency. (AC-1)
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: Add `index.ts` re-exports: explicitly OOS (§6) and contradicts the chosen convention

key_design_decisions.chosen_approach: Update ALL five moved-path sites in lockstep: UI-Primitives table (l.30), prose tier description (l.68), module-structure tree (l.83), the two code-comment markers `organisms/Shell.tsx:250` (l.136) and `organisms/Divider.tsx:250` (l.160), and the mermaid Dependency Overview (l.310)
key_design_decisions.why: AC-10 names only table + diagram, but a moved-path marker left stale is a dangling reference the feature itself creates; §3 mandates lockstep and §6.1 demands conscious-update-no-silent-drift. Updating exactly the moved-path references (line numbers unchanged — only the dir prefix moves) is the minimal set that leaves docs internally consistent.
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: Update only table+mermaid (literal AC-10): leaves 3 stale path sites, breaking the lockstep guarantee with no AC to catch it

file_impact.what_changes: Relocate; rewrite `@renderer` imports of Titlebar/PaneSplit/Statusbar to `organisms/shell/*` (Sidebar import unchanged)
Relocate; rewrite `@renderer` imports of Shell/Titlebar/Statusbar/PaneSplit → `organisms/shell/*` and Divider → `molecules/Divider`
Relocate (DISCOVERY 1 — not in spec §4); relative `./Shell.stories` import preserved by moving together; no `@renderer` rewrite needed
Stays in `organisms/__tests__/`; imports only flat TabBar
Add labelled domain-placement rule in §2.2; update §2.2:70 membership tree + §5.1:198 UI-primitives list to new layout
Update all 5 moved-path citation sites (table l.30, prose l.68, module tree l.83, markers l.136/l.160, mermaid l.310)

doc_impact.what_changes: UI-Primitives Layer table row, prose tier description, module-structure tree, two code-comment path markers (Shell.tsx / Divider.tsx), and mermaid Dependency Overview — reclassify Divider as molecule, group shell organisms under organisms/shell/

risks.risk: A moved-file `@renderer` import path is missed, breaking the renderer import graph
risks.mitigation: `typecheck:node` + `typecheck:web` (zero unresolved) and `npm run build` statically catch every dangling import; AC-11 grep asserts no old `organisms/(Divider\

risks.risk: A sibling `.css` or cross-file CSS coupling is dropped, silently degrading visuals — esp. `Tabs.stories.tsx:45` importing `Shell.css` cross-folder (the 005 brittleness)
risks.mitigation: Move each `.css` atomically with its `.tsx`; rewrite the `Tabs.stories.tsx` Shell.css import to `organisms/shell/Shell.css`; Playwright CT fidelity suite catches regression (full styling context preserved — no className/scope change)

risks.risk: Shell test trio split breaks the `./Shell.stories` relative coupling
risks.mitigation: Move all three files together to `organisms/shell/__tests__/` as one unit; keep the relative import relative (legal intra-fixture story-glue, not a cross-module §2.3 case)

risks.risk: Classification-site drift — docs code-comment markers (l.136/l.160) are NOT covered by any AC (AC-11 greps only `src/renderer`, AC-10 names only table+diagram)
risks.mitigation: Explicit task step updates all 5 moved-path sites; add a manual `grep -nE 'organisms/(Divider\

risks.risk: `@renderer`-alias regression — a rewritten import introduced as a deep relative path
risks.mitigation: All cross-module rewrites stay on the `@renderer` alias (§2.3); only the pre-existing `Shell.ct.tsx → ./Shell.stories` intra-fixture relative import is preserved relative

dependencies: None — no packages to install, no services to configure, no environment variables. The move reuses the existing electron-vite `@renderer` alias, Vitest, and Playwright CT toolchains unchanged.
